reviews.review_msg: The midterm was okay. Bro railed me on the final; the class average was a 40%. No mercy.

This class was difficult, but Steve has many resources that make passing doable, such as online lecture recordings, and premade lecture notes that you can fill as you attend. The material will be a lot to grasp, but you have access to past exams to see what is expected of you in the midterm and final. And he is very easygoing and approachable. GLHF
Avoid at all cost
i love this guy, bro is a sweet heart, mwaahhhh, old cutie pie. Make sure u study at least 3 full days ahead for the final

His lectures were pretty boring as he talks slow and I rarely went. He doesnt teach off skeleton notes like i was used to and instead teaches off of windows notebook on lined paper. Taught myself most of the material pretty easily, lots of concepts needed from 152 and 232. Final was very hard but he curved the class to an average of a b-. Nice guy.
thank god for steve's grade scaling the ta's marked my final as if i personally insulted them so stingy with those partial marks 😭

I did pretty decent in his class. His midterm was really easy, but his final had me crying on the way home. His lectures are not the best since he goes from examples then to the actual theorem. However, I still recommend going since he will talk about what is on the final or not.

He's a pretty good professor, I found his lectures boring and notes very unorganized however his quizzes and midterm were easy. Final exam was much harder and nothing like our practice, but he adjusted marks to make up for it. He's always willing to help students and seems to genuinely want the best for us, overall he's really nice.

It was going great until the final. When out of nowhere he decided to make only our final, out of all the previous finals, extremely hard. The average was 42%. And midterm's average was 73%. Still salty about why he only chose to eff up our final out of all the previous terms he has taught this course. But overall, best prof for macm 316.

Course itself was hard but Steve made it manageable. Has lecture videos from Covid and all past midterms + finals with solutions available. Computing assignments are marked strictly, and Steve doesn't explain the course material well. Don't slack off ever + make the textbook your best friend is my best advice, if you listen you will do well, GL.
Fair marker and interesting lectures. The recorded lectures were quite good as well in case you missed class.

Online video lectures and other resources like tutorials, workshops, and office hours available to help you succeed. Midterm was reasonable, computing assignments in MATLAB are difficult and graded with scrutiny, final was difficult due to it being long and including proofs. Prof. Ruuth explains concepts well, but the class is still difficult.

Steve is an okay professor. Lectures are pretty boring, and Computing assignments are pretty difficult to do. The midterm was okay, but a few tricky questions. However, the final was extremely difficult as there were lots of proofs. The average grade was a B-.

Great prof, all the content you can find video recording and gives you past finals to practice, you can replace midterm grade with final's if you get better in final, scaled the final grade a lot in this sem, assignments are not that hard but the page limitation kinda annoying, sadly this sem's final is at least 3x harder than past finals.
bit hard marking computing assignments, BUT HES THE GOAT… THE GOAAAAATTT !!! EXAM JAIL , THOUGHT I GOT RAILED, BUT AN INSANE SCALE…. LAKERS IN 5
I never wore my seatbelt going to class because i hoped something would hit me on the way

Lectures are filled with proof I could not understand but too nice of a grader, Final grade was scaled up by two letter grades. Exams are not too bad but the way he teaches is really frustrating, good thing that this course has a lot of resources online

The lectures are less than stellar, and usually not particularly helpful. Assignments are vague and poorly worded, and then marked strictly. Overall average professor. I would only take him again if I had to.

The prof can have messy handwriting at times. But he is pretty respectful and chill. Tests and assignments were also marked fairly, based on what was learned in the course, and the tutorials were super helpful in showing the steps used in working towards solutions for questions.

This is a tough class. His notes can be a bit unorganized and I found the grading to be very tough (not a lot of part marks). But I feel like he tried to explain things as best as he could and he was a nice enough guy. Not a bad choice for MACM 316. Hopefully he switches to using slides more.

1) Online pre-recorded videos will be 95% of the source of information in this course. 
2) Each 15-20 min video will take around 1-3 hours to effectively learn. 
3) Most of the learning is done by self-study. Very little guidance is there from the professor.
4) Do all the previous year's exams, that will be provided (LEARN THEM FOR FINAL EXAM)

I thought that his lectures were good at explaining the content. He also made online lectures available which was helpful. He seems pretty accommodating with marking. My main criticism is that his handwriting can be quite messy which makes it difficult to understand what he is writing. Otherwise a very solid professor.

Professor Ruuth is an odd professor with a soft tone of voice which can make the material seem boring. He is disorganized but extremely approachable outside of class. He makes lots of mistakes which made me wonder if he truly knows the material. Overall good professor for boosting your grade.

GO. TO. OFFICE HOURS!!! His lectures are indeed useless, however the lecture notes themselves are very detailed and easy to read for a quick review. I do wish he included more examples in notes so we could be exposed to some complex questions. In office hours he was quite good at explaining questions, although his writing is messy. Very hard tests!

I really wasn't a big fan of his 6 tests instead of midterms and a final format because each test felt like a midterm anyway so it just felt like a lot more work. Each test was also only out of 16 so if fail a question you lose a lot of points. Lectures were useless, basically a 50 min lecture squeezed into 15 min, but discussion hours were helpful
A test out of 16 marks worth like 18% of the final grade is messed up

Lectures are pretty meh, with concepts explained really densely and indecipherable writing. Grading on the other hand is pretty great. You get plenty of time to think of answers and check over them, and marks are evenly spread across the top 5/6 tests, so one big screw up won't ruin your term. No trick questions on tests either.

His synchronous class as stated turned into only office hours and recorded videos. His recorded videos were hard to understand due to his handwriting. Didn't answer questions that well during office hour. Recommend to take this class only if you're willing to look for other videos to understand material

6 tests, 5 computing assignments. Drops the worse grade for 1 test & 1 CA. Tests and CA alternate by each week. Questions on the tests were similar to that of the homework/tutorials (do them, not many). CA's were a little convoluted at times. Course known to be tough, Dr. Ruuth made the course easily passable. Lectures/notes not great, use txtbook
The worst lectures and writtings I have ever seen

- messy writings - bad lectures (no live lectures, low quality videos, short, no explanation, skipping details) - make hard tests to trick students - reduce your grade for minor reasons even if you get the right answer (significant digits, some random requirements not even listed in the question, etc)

Honestly he was a great professor to have for calc 252, I had to retake this course because I didn't get a good enough graded the first time around. He never threw curveballs and everything was pretty standard. My only complaint was that it was hard to read his handwriting lol. BUT I felt he was extremely fair and really set us up well for tests:)

Steve is the perfect guy for MACM316. I really enjoyed the way he made his notes. Do every homework question and ask your friends if they got last term quizzes because he dont change them. You can score an easy A if you will do homework, do all the last term midterms. Final was all homework questions and proofs that are all in notes. Do all proofs

This class is very tough and quite boring, but Steve did a good job explaining concepts and trying to pass on his enthusiasm of the topics. Midterm and final were tough, the average for the midterm was 51%. If you pay attention in lecture and really understand the concepts presented, and the examples shown then you have a chance at a decent grade.
I enjoyed this class so much I took it twice.
